name: Rust-Flake Calibrator
description: A thumb-sized brass dial packed with iron sediment scraped from old mine-cart axles.

name: Brass Drip Coupling
description: A repurposed valve fitting from the Goblinn cellar pipes, releasing consistent mineral drips.

name: Pickle-Line Tubing Section
description: A stained coil of tubing once used in Taffy’s pickle-transfer setup; still smells sharp.

name: Salinity Gauge Cartridge
description: A small brass sensor cartridge pieced from broken boiler gauges, needle twitching erratically.

name: Tap-Valve Concentrator
description: A modified tavern tap valve that condenses trace salts as liquid passes through.

name: Dust of Minto Horn
description: Finely ground powder from the mineral-loaded horn of a deep-tunnel Minto beast.

name: Pickle-Brine Filter Disk
description: A mesh disk used to strain seeds, spice, and solids from Taffy’s pickle vats.

name: Minewater Compression Brick
description: A dense sediment brick formed by years of mineral pressure in abandoned tunnels.

name: Electro-Salt Capacitor
description: A charged brass canister built from tavern lighting fixtures and cellar wiring, humming with mineral tension.

name: Outhouse Sludge Tablet
description: A composting tablet from the Goblinn outhouse repurposed to modulate ionic balance when ground and distilled.

name: Cellar Salt-Press Plate
description: A heavy plate once used to compact salt and cheese in the Goblinn’s cellar press, still crusted with brine.

name: Iono-Regulation Core
description: A sealed orb built from brass lamp housings and deep-vein mineral fragments, regulating salinity with uncanny precision.

name: House-Linen Mineral Strip
description: A strip of laundry-room towel fabric stiffened by decades of hard-water mineral crust.

name: pH Drip Regulator
description: A salvage from the brewery filtration rigs, now repurposed to deliver controlled neutralizing drips.

name: Neutralizing Valve Pellet
description: A corrosion-moderating bead originally used in mine pump housings.

name: Vinegar-Stone Bite
description: A small stone chunk gnawed by Vinegar-Bats, soaked in naturally acidic saliva.

name: Balancing Basin Cartridge
description: A ceramic filter cartridge taken from an old dwarven water-tuning basin.

name: Metered Dose Basin Syringe
description: A stainless dosing syringe rebuilt from dishwashing-line tools, meant for tiny, exact fluid adjustments.

name: Unicorn Brew Settling Disc
description: A disc used to separate foam layers in Unicorn Brew kettles; coated with reactive sweet-acid residue.

name: Dairy-Ladle pH Paddle
description: A wooden ladle used in goat-milk prep, its surface etched with residue perfect for fine pH adjustments.

name: Guestroom Basin Scale Chip
description: A mineral flake chipped from the underside of old guestroom washbasins, packed with layered deposits.

name: Basin-Reactor Flask
description: A squat flask reinforced with laundry-starch films and copper banding to stabilize volatile mixtures.

name: Mineral pH Regulator Shard
description: A calcite shard infused with glowing cavern growths, vibrating with subtle chemical weight.

name: Volatility Modulation Tube
description: A copper-lined modulator tube scavenged from the brewing line, balancing unstable reactions via controlled swirl.

name: Deep-Clean Scraper Head
description: A heavy scraper attachment from the inn’s cleaning closet, once used to descale brewing and septic pipes.

name: pH Equilibrium Lance
description: A long brass fluid-lance built from stove piping and pump housings, achieving near-perfect chemical alignment.

name: Flux-Wick Assembly
description: A wick woven from stove-lamp fibers and cellar threads, responsive to ambient flux.

name: Conduction Scrap Chip
description: A shard taken from an old conduction plate on the mine elevators.

name: Stove Coil Topper
description: A warped topper salvaged from the Goblinn’s kitchen stovetops.

name: Pantry Thermo-Crank
description: A hand-crank from a broken food dehydrator in pantry storage, still turning slightly warm.

name: Boiler-Runoff Coil Segment
description: A coil fragment taken from the inn’s basement boiler system, still resonant with residual warmth cycling.

name: Cellar-Core Capsule
description: A capsule lined with cold cellar moss, used to stabilize low-temp food stores.

name: Generator-Chain Flux Loop
description: A smooth metal loop once part of the Goblinn generator’s chain assembly, repurposed to even out fluctuating energy patte...

name: Goat-Stall Comfort Rod
description: A rod used to maintain stable environmental comfort around goat feed troughs during unpredictable temperature swings.

name: Bedframe Coil Support
description: A spring coil salvaged from a creaky guestroom bedframe, its tension now tuned for equilibrium functions.

name: Kinetic-Whip Rod
description: A wrapped alloy rod reacting dynamically to flickering environmental energy.

name: Charge-Burst Pebble
description: A pebble from cavern fauna that stores tiny bursts of reactive spark-energy.

name: Stabilizer Core Block
description: A cold-core block holding structured energy patterns.

name: Smokehouse Ember Disk
description: A metal plate from the Goblinn smokehouse floor, simmering with old heat and cured oils.

name: Flux Convergence Node
description: A multi-layered disk driving temperature into equilibrium.

name: Chime-Plate Resonator
description: A thin bell plate salvaged from the tavern’s old stage chime.

name: Buzz-Coil Relay
description: A compact relay coil scavenged from mine alarm systems.

name: Vibe-Spring Coupling
description: A jittering spring connector built from dishwashing rack hardware.

name: Broom-Handle Resonance Rod
description: A broken broom handle wrapped in copper wire by bored Goblinn staff.

name: Stove-Plate Resonance Cage
description: A resonance housing built from warped kitchen stove plates and iron bands.

name: Tuning Fork Assembly
description: Twin prongs mounted on a brass bracket once used by tavern musicians to test harmonics.

name: Bar-Top Acoustic Rod
description: A tuning rod used behind the bar for testing glassware resonance.

name: Maintenance Rattle Clamp
description: A clamp used to identify rattling ductwork.

name: Door-Hinge Resonance Pin
description: A hinge-pin from the noisy hallway doors, polished by decades of use into a perfect vibration guide.

name: Bottling Conveyor Harmonic Wheel
description: A rotating wheel salvaged from the Unicorn Brew bottling line, etched with harmonic grooves.

name: Lantern-Soot Oscillation Baffle
description: A brass baffle scraped from tavern lanterns, coated in soot that vibrates in perfect patterns.

name: Chroma Conduction Core
description: A prismatic core fused with chromatic beast-scale fragments.

name: Stage-Bell Resonance Drum
description: A mini-drum from the Goblinn stage, perfect for overtones.

name: Boiler-Gauge Flux Coupler
description: A multi-chambered resonant coupler assembled from broken boiler gauges and barrel hoops, collapsing vibrations into perf...

name: The Ancient Titration Stone
description: A palm stone carved with channels worn smooth by centuries of experimental pouring. Warm to the touch at random, as if r...

name: The Brine-Eater’s Coil
description: A twisted copper helix found beneath the tavern’s foundation, humming faintly like it’s digesting something unseen.

name: The Unsplit Hourglass
description: A sealed glass vessel where the sand refuses to fall unless held sideways. No Gob has ever figured out why.

name: The Whisper-Jar Fragment
description: A shard of thick glass from an unknown container — when tapped, it echoes with distant clinks, as if something is inside...

name: The Half-Melted Calibration Crown
description: A crown-shaped ring of metal partially melted but still impossibly symmetrical. Engineers hate it; wizards fear it.

name: The Bound-Spiral Lens
description: A lens wrapped in metal wiring that shifts color depending on which Gob is looking through it.

name: The Forgotten Resonance Nail
description: A long rusted nail that vibrates at night. Staff keep trying to throw it out, but it always reappears somewhere useful.

name: The Forge-Dormant Ember Core
description: A small cold ember that still leaves scorch marks when placed near parchment.

name: The Goat-Stall Miracle Rag
description: A filthy cleaning rag that somehow removes exactly the stain you weren’t aiming at. A stable miracle.

name: Quiet-Gift Counterpulse Rod
description: A simple brass rod left on a tavern table one night by an unknown guest—never explained, never claimed—and most guests c...
